confirmed_interpretation.label: pion two-point correlator
confirmed_interpretation.summary: User explicitly asked for a pion two-point correlator.

convention_decisions.decision: Use wall source at a specified source timeslice.
convention_decisions.why: The fixed v1 workflow follows the local meson-spec test pattern for wall-source propagator generation.

convention_decisions.decision: Use the gamma5-based zero-momentum pion contraction written with CuPy einsum.
convention_decisions.why: The contraction shape and gamma-matrix usage are grounded in the local meson-spec test and pion example, with gamma helpers from pyquda_utils.gamma.

convention_decisions.decision: Read the input gauge with io.readQIOGauge from a Chroma/QIO gauge path.
convention_decisions.why: The fixed workflow uses the same QIO gauge-loading path exercised in the local IO test and helper module.

convention_decisions.decision: Restrict v1 to a single pion two-point correlator at zero momentum.
convention_decisions.why: The local examples separate zero-momentum and dispersion workflows; the external citations justify the narrow two-point zero-momentum convention.

external_citations.chosen_convention: Use a Euclidean two-point correlator with interpolating operators chosen to overlap the target pion channel at zero momentum.
external_citations.knowledge_boundary_note: This citation record comes from a local curated JSON artifact, not from a live online lookup during the current run.
external_citations.title: Hadron Spectroscopy with Lattice QCD
external_citations.why_needed: PyQUDA examples and tests show the implementation pattern, but this review provides the lattice-spectroscopy framing for the correlator and operator-selection convention.

external_citations.chosen_convention: Use the narrow wall-source zero-momentum convention and record that the p=0 truncation corresponds to wall sources.
external_citations.knowledge_boundary_note: This citation record comes from a local curated JSON artifact, not from a live online lookup during the current run.
external_citations.title: The sigma meson from lattice QCD with two-pion interpolating operators
external_citations.why_needed: PyQUDA local references encode wall inversions and zero-momentum contractions but do not explain the physics-side meaning of the wall-source p=0 choice.
